mission.title: Upgrade KubeStellar Console
mission.description: Performs a safe rolling upgrade of your current KubeStellar Console helm release to a newer version while persisting active configurations and backing up current defaults.

steps.title: Refresh Helm Repositories
steps.description:
Sync local helm repositories with upstream to find the latest published charts.
```bash
helm repo update kubestellar-console
```

steps.title: Backup Existing Configurations
steps.description:
Extract active configurations from the current deployment so they are not lost during upgrade.
```bash
helm get values kc -n kubestellar-console > custom-values-backup.yaml
```

steps.title: Execute Rolling Upgrade
steps.description:
Launch the upgrade passing in the preserved values from the previous step.
```bash
helm upgrade kc kubestellar-console/kubestellar-console --namespace kubestellar-console --values custom-values-backup.yaml --wait --timeout 120s
```

steps.title: Verify Rollout Status
steps.description:
Ensure that the replica set has fulfilled and your deployment is running successfully.
```bash
kubectl rollout status deployment kc-kubestellar-console -n kubestellar-console
# Wait for pods 
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=kc -n kubestellar-console --timeout=90s
```

resolution.summary: KubeStellar Console upgraded successfully. If anomalies persist post-upgrade, verify application logs or use the rollback feature.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Console
uninstall.description:
Remove the helm release alongside its scoped namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall kc -n kubestellar-console
```

troubleshooting.title: Revert to Previous State
troubleshooting.description:
If your deployed release is misbehaving, you can reliably revert back a revision instantly via helm rollback.
```bash
helm rollback kc -n kubestellar-console
```

prerequisites.description: A pre-existing KubeStellar Console installation must exist within the `kubestellar-console` namespace via helm release `kc`.
